caption: A Tokyo coffee shop caught sideways, the world still moving through it.
Two friends mid-conversation, a yellow truck passing behind them like punctuation.
A late-night produce market in Japan, colour louder than the hour.
Beneath a tangle of wires and signage, she reaches for something just out of frame.
Hands peeling a mandarin slowly, the evening asking nothing more of anyone.
A red postbox beside hydrangeas, as if the flowers had something to send.
A curved bridge glimpsed through branches, half-hidden and unhurried.
A white rose let itself go, petals scattered like a quiet admission.
Pink roses past their peak, still insisting on being seen.
